reviews.review_title: Best free app on introductory Astronomy
reviews.review_description: This is a great app! I’m really looking forward to the full version. What would be great is an opportunity to fly the LEM onto the moon’s surface. I also love how you can walk around within the models to explore. Very impressive and informative. So far, the models and information seem accurate. Kudos to the developers. Well done!

reviews.review_title: MOST EXCITING FREE APP in 3 years time
reviews.review_description: SPACE ATLAS VR is a captivating virtual reality application that offers a rich, immersive exploration of the cosmos. This app allows users to compare climate changes across planets, explore landscapes on the Moon and Mars, and interact with space telescopes. With features like the ability to manipulate and rotate planets for detailed examination, a virtual tour inside the lunar module, and an interactive star map, SPACE ATLAS VR provides a comprehensive educational experience. Its extensive, interactive tutorials make learning engaging and accessible, and the app’s flexibility allows it to be used with or without controllers. Currently free through the App Lab program and available on iPhones and iPads via the Meta Horizon app, SPACE ATLAS VR is a must-try for astronomy enthusiasts, offering high-quality content that combines entertainment with education.

I love this app it's fairly easy to use and I get lost in it
I can't wait to be able to land on more planets and moons 
My only gripe is the graphics are not so good but hopefully will be better in future updates 
Also I would love to be able to move around on the planets and moons 
Great work devs keep on updating 
This app would be worth paying for premium updates

An app that is similar to TITAN OF SPACE+ without ever equaling it but with some interesting specific advantages.
- Hand tracking control for greater immersion.
- “Visit” of the soil of the planets.
- 3D models "exploded" in under-cases.
It is a young application which will grow and which has the potential to become a reference with still a lot of work.
The graphics are good but deserve more care.
The tutorial is already clear and quite complete.
NOTE TO DEVS
Consider adding a sit/stand mode or an option to adjust the height.

This app gets better update after update.
Very well built and informative, a must have for everyone, even more for astronomy lovers ❤️
The possibility to use the controllers was a nice touch too.

Very educational. Once I got familiar with navigating through the app I  started to like it. Nice views. Handtracking works fine for me. Feedback: I find the panes with background information difficult to read. Small letters in a not so good resolution. I am on the Q2. Question: how do I exit the application? I do not see an exit option which means i need the controllers to exit.Looking forward to further development!

Solid handtracking and nice contents that I'm sure will be completed in the final version.
Nice sense of scale and easy navigation between sections.
Da aspera ad astra. Keep up the good work!
Tried this app but for the love of God I can't get this handtracking working properly. Wish they would allow normal controllers.

developer_response.body: Robair996, thank you for your feedback. Our aim is to create an extremely immersive experience, with controls as intuitive as possible. This is the reason why we have decided to go for hand tracking only. Your feedback is very welcome but quite surprising to us, because the feedback from our early betatester has been always positive in terms of usability. Can you please provide examples of the issues you are facing with hand tracking? It would be extremely helpful to improve our project.
